mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over recent months signals easier policy, lower discount rates, and higher near-term equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is declining.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates enough to keep FEDFUNDS restrictive, real discount rates rise and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% m/m for 2 consecutive months and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the inflation-policy interaction is not negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a stable labor-market baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which lowers earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not negative following the specified unemployment rise in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the effect disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS implies stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue momentum, which supports earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly or weekly changes in RSAFS fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign flips negative in a rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO after a prior weakness phase indicates improving industrial output and profit-sensitive cyclical activity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases for 2 consecutive months after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if recoveries in INDPRO are followed by negative 60-day returns more than 40% of the time, or if the effect is absent when GDP growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts strength combined with firm retail sales indicates synchronized household demand, which supports equity earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise month-over-month for 2 straight months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-RSAFS signal does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered cases show negative 60-day returns in a majority of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration that reduces forward earnings and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 months while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration fails to predict negative 20-day returns better than chance, or if the sign is not negative after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDP and employment together confirm real activity reacceleration, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with simultaneous GDPC1 and PAYEMS improvement do not produce positive 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the joint signal is weaker than either proxy alone.
